criteria.description: Reusable sidecar runtime service handler accepts raw purple.sidecar.v1 durable-resume request envelopes and returns encoded response envelopes.
criteria.notes: SidecarRuntimeService::handle() decodes raw envelope JSON with SidecarProtocol and returns encoded SidecarResumeResponse envelopes.

criteria.description: Service handler loads durable run records through DurableRunStore and returns deterministic accepted/rejected responses.
criteria.notes: Tests cover accepted existing runs, missing_run rejection, unsupported_action rejection, and malformed envelope failure.

criteria.description: Existing bin/purple CLI exposes a local sidecar resume prototype command.
criteria.notes: Command shape is purple sidecar resume <run-store-dir> <run-id> [node-id], with JSON output and rejected-run exit status.

criteria.description: Runtime service and CLI prototype behavior are covered by tests.
criteria.evidence: vendor/bin/phpunit -c phpunit.xml.dist tests/Runtime/Sidecar/SidecarRuntimeServiceTest.php; vendor/bin/phpunit -c phpunit.xml.dist tests/Cli/PurpleCliTest.php; composer check
criteria.notes: Focused WP tests and integrated composer check cover service behavior, CLI success, missing-run output, and CLI usage errors.

criteria.description: Sidecar runtime docs and examples explain the envelope contract, CLI command, and local-store expectations.
criteria.notes: Docs describe SidecarRuntimeService, deterministic response outcomes, and the local durable store command sequence.

criteria.description: Roadmap and architecture docs represent Phase 5.4 as completed sidecar runtime prototype work.
criteria.notes: Docs mark Phase 5.4 complete and keep native compatibility, provider publication, and future package splits separate.

criteria.description: Mission evidence proves Composer-mode root validation still passes without required sidecar, native, cloud SDK, AWS, or live network dependencies.
criteria.notes: Validation evidence keeps the sidecar prototype optional and local; no daemon, socket listener, native extension, cloud SDK, AWS credential, or live service is required.
